assumptions: The recognised intragroup dividend receivable is a monetary item denominated in a currency different from at least one relevant group entity's functional currency.
The related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and therefore affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup receivable can be designated for hedge accounting in consolidation under the specific IFRS 9 exception for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items, subject to formal designation and documentation.
